title: ReplacementScoreboard
description: Set 069 S5 - the RAW scoreboard of the automated verification surface scored against a pre-registered benchmark (benchmark-registration.json). Written as replacement-scoreboard.json. Carries the per-case outcomes (detected / replayed / falsely-REPRODUCED), the spurious-detection count (the precision denominator's false positives), and the gated-surface telemetry the Set 068 DEMOTE decision said RETIRE reopens on. It carries NO verdict: ai_router.replacement_gate.score_benchmark derives recall/precision/replay-success/false-REPRODUCED and the cadence recommendation from the registration + this scoreboard, so the verdict is derived, never hand-asserted. The runtime contract is the pure-Python validator ai_router.replacement_gate.validate_scoreboard.

spuriousDetections.description: Detections that correspond to no registered case (false positives - the precision denominator). Default 0.

outcomes.description: Per-case outcomes. Each caseId must be a registered case (score_benchmark rejects unregistered ids).

detected.description: Did the automated surface catch this defect?

replayed.description: Of a detected defect, did it produce a valid pristine replay (a re-runnable falsifier)?

falseReproduced.description: Integrity flag recorded by the scorer: did this detection carry a REPRODUCED tag that was NOT actually backed by a sound pristine replay (a false REPRODUCED)? Recorded independently of `replayed` -- a replay may have run yet validated the wrong thing (a meta-oracle failure), so this is not derived from `replayed == false`.

GatedSurfaceTelemetry.description: The telemetry of the gated verification surface - the data the Set 068 DEMOTE decision said RETIRE reopens on.

escapedDefectRate.description: Fraction of real defects that escaped the gated surface (in [0, 1]).

falsePositiveChurn.description: False-positive churn (a non-negative rate or count of spurious findings the surface produced).

predicateShouldHaveFiredMisses.description: Count of cases where the blast-radius predicate should have fired but did not.

reworkSaved.description: Optional measure of rework saved (e.g. defects caught at intro stage rather than end-of-set).

introStageCatches.description: Defects caught at the intro (in-snippet) stage.

endOfSetCatches.description: Defects caught only at the end-of-set (cross-file) stage.
